class RoFormerTokenizerFast(PreTrainedTokenizerFast):
    r"""
    Construct a "fast" RoFormer tokenizer (backed by HuggingFace's *tokenizers* library).

    [`RoFormerTokenizerFast`] is almost identical to [`BertTokenizerFast`] and runs end-to-end tokenization:
    punctuation splitting and wordpiece. There are some difference between them when tokenizing Chinese.

    This tokenizer inherits from [`PreTrainedTokenizerFast`] which contains most of the main methods. Users should
    refer to this superclass for more information regarding those methods.

    Example:

    ```python
    >>> from transformers import RoFormerTokenizerFast

# FastText Language Identification

This is a language identification model trained on the [Wikipedia Language Identification Dataset](https://github.com/pvkr/wikilangid).

The model is trained using [FastText](https://fasttext.cc/docs/en/language-identification.html).

## Usage

```python
from fasttext import load_model

model = load_model("lid.176.bin")
model.predict("This is a test sentence.")
```

## License

This model is licensed under the [CC-BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/) license.

# FastText Language Identification

This is a language identification model trained on the [Wikipedia Language Identification Dataset](https://github.com/pvkr/wikilangid).

The model is trained using [FastText](https://fasttext.cc/docs/en/language-identification.html).

## Usage

```python
from fasttext import load_model

# Load the pre-trained model
model = load_model("lid.176.bin")

# Predict the language of a text
text = "This is a test sentence."
predictions = model.predict(text)

print(predictions)
# Example output: (('__label__en',), (0.9987,))
# Format: (labels, probabilities)
```

## Model Details
- **Model Name**: `lid.176.bin`
- **Languages Supported**: 176
- **Training Data**: Wikipedia extracts
- **License**: CC-BY-SA 4.0

## License

This model is licensed under the [CC-BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/) license.

---

### How to use this model
If you have the `lid.176.bin` file, you can run the code above. If you don't have the model file yet, you can download it from the official FastText repository:

```bash
wget https://dl.fbaipublicfiles.com/fasttext/supervised-models/lid.176.bin
```

Let me know if you need help training a custom model or processing specific languages!
